title: scenario_ransomware_encrypt
description: Ransomware propagates across the corporate network, encrypting files on workstations and shared drives.

timeline.title: Endpoint Alert Triggered
timeline.description: Endpoint protection flags unusual mass file-rename activity on a Finance workstation. User reports files unreadable.

timeline.title: Encryption Spreading
timeline.description: Four additional workstations reporting identical symptoms. Shared drive access is returning garbled files.

timeline.title: Network Shares Targeted
timeline.description: Encryption spreading to mapped network drives. Critical project files and HR records affected.

timeline.title: Ransom Note Discovered
timeline.description: README_RESTORE.txt found on every encrypted folder. Attackers demand 40 BTC within 72 hours.

timeline.title: Backup Status Check
timeline.description: IT verifying offline backup integrity. Last verified clean backup was taken 18 hours ago.

timeline.title: Recovery Underway
timeline.description: Restoration from air-gapped backup initiated. Affected systems being rebuilt from clean images.

timeline.title: Post-Incident Review
timeline.description: Systems restored. Lessons-learned session scheduled. Backup cadence and EDR rules under review.

node-1.title: Endpoint Alert: Mass File Encryption
node-1.situation: Your endpoint detection tool alerts on mass file-rename activity consistent with ransomware on a Finance workstation. The affected user says files are unreadable and a README_RESTORE.txt has appeared on the desktop.
node-1.question: What is your immediate action?

options.label: A
options.text: Isolate the workstation from the network immediately by disabling its NIC, then alert the IR team.

options.label: B
options.text: Run an antivirus scan on the machine while it stays connected to diagnose the infection first.

options.label: C
options.text: Reboot the workstation to see if the activity stops.

node-2.title: Multiple Systems Affected
node-2.situation: Four additional workstations are exhibiting the same symptoms. The ransomware is propagating via mapped network drives. The IR team is assembled and awaiting direction.
node-2.question: How do you contain the outbreak?

options.label: A
options.text: Segment the affected VLAN by disabling the switch ports for all infected machines and block SMB traffic at the firewall.

options.label: B
options.text: Shut down only the originally infected workstation and continue monitoring the others.

options.label: C
options.text: Take the entire network offline to halt all activity.

node-3.title: Ransom Demand Received
node-3.situation: Containment is holding. Attackers demand 40 BTC within 72 hours. Management is asking whether to pay. The CISO notes no guarantee of decryption keys being delivered.
node-3.question: How do you advise management on the ransom demand?

options.label: A
options.text: Do not pay the ransom. Assess backup availability and engage law enforcement. Document everything for regulatory reporting.

options.label: B
options.text: Pay the ransom immediately to restore operations as quickly as possible.

node-4.title: Backup Integrity Verification
node-4.situation: IT has located an air-gapped backup from 18 hours ago. Before restoring, you must verify it is clean and unaffected by the ransomware.
node-4.question: How do you validate the backup before restoration?

options.label: A
options.text: Mount the backup on an isolated, clean test system, run integrity checks, and scan for malware signatures before restoring production.

options.label: B
options.text: Restore directly from backup to production systems without testing to minimize downtime.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: Backup integrity confirmed. Systems restored from clean images. Law enforcement notified. A post-incident review is scheduled to improve detection rules, backup cadence, and staff awareness training.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
